# Nopal

> Cross-platform UI framework for OCaml using MVU (Model-View-Update) architecture. Build local-first applications targeting desktop, mobile, and web from a single OCaml codebase.

Nopal compiles to JavaScript via js_of_ocaml for web and Tauri webview targets. Desktop and mobile are served via Tauri. The framework is not a React-style VDOM framework — it uses a pure description type (`Element.t`) that renderers interpret directly.

Homepage: nopal.run

## Architecture

Every concern is a separate opam package with strict one-way dependency flow:

```
nopal_mvu       ← MVU core (Cmd, Sub, App) — no UI or platform deps
nopal_element   ← Element.t DSL — no platform deps
nopal_style     ← Typed layout/paint — no platform deps
nopal_router    ← Type-safe bidirectional routing — no platform deps
nopal_scene     ← Pure scene types (Color, Paint, Transform, Path segments, Scene) — no platform deps
nopal_draw      ← 2D drawing DSL (Scale + higher-level Path algorithms) — depends on nopal_scene
nopal_svg       ← Pure SVG 1.1 string renderer for nopal_scene — no platform deps
nopal_charts    ← Composable interactive charts (Bar, Line, Area, Pie, Scatter, Sparkline,
                   Heat_map, Trading.Candlestick, Trading.Drawdown, Chart_pane,
                   Color_scale, Domain_window, Viewport, Downsample, Snap)
nopal_http      ← Pure HTTP types + backend abstraction — no platform deps
nopal_ui        ← High-level UI components (Button, Checkbox, Data_table, Modal, Navigation_bar, Radio, Select, Toast) — no platform deps
nopal_runtime   ← Lwd reactive integration + MVU loop
nopal_test      ← Pure OCaml test renderer — no js_of_ocaml
nopal_web       ← js_of_ocaml + Brr DOM renderer (only browser-dependent package)
nopal_http_web  ← Browser Fetch API backend for nopal_http — js_of_ocaml + Brr
nopal_tauri     ← Tauri desktop/mobile bindings — depends on nopal_mvu (for Task.t), nopal_router (for Platform.S)
```

Critical boundary: `nopal_element`, `nopal_style`, `nopal_test`, `nopal_router` must compile on native OCaml without js_of_ocaml. Application view code never imports `nopal_web` or `Brr`.

## Quick Start

### Setup

```bash
opam install . --deps-only --with-test
just build    # opam exec -- dune build
just test     # opam exec -- dune runtest
just          # build + test + fmt + lint (run before every commit)
```

### Minimal Counter App

**counter.ml** — Pure model, update, and view:

```ocaml
open Nopal_element
open Nopal_style

type model = { count : int }
type msg = Increment | Decrement | Reset

let init () = ({ count = 0 }, Nopal_mvu.Cmd.none)

let update model = function
  | Increment -> ({ count = model.count + 1 }, Nopal_mvu.Cmd.none)
  | Decrement -> ({ count = max 0 (model.count - 1) }, Nopal_mvu.Cmd.none)
  | Reset -> ({ count = 0 }, Nopal_mvu.Cmd.none)

let button_style =
  Style.default
  |> Style.with_layout (fun l ->
       { l with width = Some (Fixed 64.0) } |> Style.padding 8.0 16.0 8.0 16.0)

let view model =
  column
    ~style:
      (Style.default
      |> Style.with_layout (fun l ->
           { l with cross_align = Some Center; gap = Some 8.0 } |> Style.padding_all 16.0))
    [
      text (string_of_int model.count);
      button ~style:button_style ~on_click:Increment (text "+");
      button ~style:button_style ~on_click:Decrement (text "-");
      button ~style:button_style ~on_click:Reset (text "Reset");
    ]

let subscriptions _model = Nopal_mvu.Sub.none
```

**main.ml** — Web mounting (the only file that touches browser APIs):

```ocaml
let () =
  let open Brr in
  let target =
    match Document.find_el_by_id G.document (Jstr.v "app") with
    | Some el -> el
    | None ->
        let body = Document.body G.document in
        let div = El.div [] in
        El.append_children body [ div ];
        div
  in
  Nopal_web.mount
    (module Counter : Nopal_mvu.App.S
      with type model = Counter.model
       and type msg = Counter.msg)
    target
```

## Core API Reference

### App Module (`nopal_mvu`)

Every Nopal application implements this signature:

```ocaml
module type App.S = sig
  type model
  type msg
  val init          : unit -> model * msg Cmd.t
  val update        : model -> msg -> model * msg Cmd.t
  val view          : model -> msg Element.t
  val subscriptions : model -> msg Sub.t
end
```

### Commands (`Cmd.t`)

Pure descriptions of side effects — never execute directly. The runtime interprets them.

```ocaml
Cmd.none                                      (* no effect *)
Cmd.batch : 'msg Cmd.t list -> 'msg Cmd.t     (* combine multiple commands *)
Cmd.perform : (('msg -> unit) -> unit) -> 'msg Cmd.t  (* general effect — zero, one, or many dispatches *)
Cmd.task : 'msg Task.t -> 'msg Cmd.t                  (* async task effect *)
Cmd.after : int -> 'msg -> 'msg Cmd.t         (* delay message by N ms *)
Cmd.map : ('a -> 'b) -> 'a Cmd.t -> 'b Cmd.t  (* transform message type *)
```

### Tasks (`Task.t`)

Composable async operations — a continuation monad. Tasks are pure descriptions; they do not execute until `run` is called. Use `let*` / `let+` syntax from `Task.Syntax` for readable chaining.

```ocaml
type 'a Task.t                                         (* async operation producing 'a *)

Task.return : 'a -> 'a Task.t                          (* wrap a pure value *)
Task.from_callback : (('a -> unit) -> unit) -> 'a Task.t  (* bridge callback-style async *)
Task.map  : ('a -> 'b) -> 'a Task.t -> 'b Task.t      (* transform result *)
Task.bind : ('a -> 'b Task.t) -> 'a Task.t -> 'b Task.t   (* sequence two tasks *)
Task.run  : 'a Task.t -> ('a -> unit) -> unit          (* execute and observe result *)

(* Cancellation *)
type Task.cancellation_token
Task.cancellable : (Task.cancellation_token -> 'a Task.t)
  -> Task.cancellation_token * ('a, string) result Task.t
Task.cancel         : Task.cancellation_token -> unit       (* idempotent *)
Task.set_on_cancel  : Task.cancellation_token -> (unit -> unit) -> unit
Task.is_cancelled   : Task.cancellation_token -> bool

(* Syntax — open Task.Syntax for let operators *)
module Task.Syntax : sig
  val ( let* ) : 'a Task.t -> ('a -> 'b Task.t) -> 'b Task.t   (* bind *)
  val ( let+ ) : 'a Task.t -> ('a -> 'b) -> 'b Task.t          (* map *)
end
```

### Subscriptions (`Sub.t`)

Ongoing event sources managed by the runtime. All subscriptions are keyed for identity-based diffing.

```ocaml
Sub.none                                                        (* no subs *)
Sub.batch : 'msg Sub.t list -> 'msg Sub.t                       (* combine *)
Sub.every : string -> int -> (unit -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t       (* timer, key + ms *)
Sub.on_keydown : string -> (string -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t       (* global keydown *)
Sub.on_keyup : string -> (string -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t         (* global keyup *)
Sub.on_resize : string -> (int -> int -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t    (* window resize *)
Sub.on_visibility_change : string -> (bool -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t
Sub.custom : string -> (('msg -> unit) -> unit -> unit) -> 'msg Sub.t  (* setup returns cleanup *)
Sub.map : ('a -> 'b) -> 'a Sub.t -> 'b Sub.t
```

### Element DSL (`nopal_element`)

`Element.t` is an exposed (not abstract) variant type so renderers can pattern-match exhaustively.

```ocaml
Element.empty                                                    (* renders nothing *)
Element.text : string -> 'msg Element.t                          (* plain text *)
Element.styled_text : text_style:Text.t -> string -> 'msg Element.t

(* Layout containers — Box supports pointer + wheel events for pan/zoom *)
type pointer_event = { x : float; y : float; client_x : float; client_y : float }
type wheel_event = { delta_y : float; x : float; y : float }

Element.box    : ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t -> ?attrs:(string * string) list
  -> ?on_pointer_move:(pointer_event -> 'msg) -> ?on_pointer_leave:'msg
  -> ?on_pointer_down:(pointer_event -> 'msg) -> ?on_pointer_up:(pointer_event -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_wheel:(wheel_event -> 'msg) -> 'msg Element.t list -> 'msg Element.t
Element.row    : ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t -> ?attrs:(string * string) list -> 'msg Element.t list -> 'msg Element.t
Element.column : ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t -> ?attrs:(string * string) list -> 'msg Element.t list -> 'msg Element.t

(* Interactive elements *)
Element.button : ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t -> ?attrs:(string * string) list
  -> ?on_click:'msg -> ?on_dblclick:'msg -> 'msg Element.t -> 'msg Element.t
Element.input : ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t -> ?attrs:(string * string) list
  -> ?placeholder:string -> ?on_change:(string -> 'msg) -> ?on_submit:'msg
  -> ?on_blur:'msg -> ?on_keydown:(string -> 'msg option) -> string -> 'msg Element.t

(* Media and scrolling *)
Element.image  : ?style:Style.t -> src:string -> alt:string -> unit -> 'msg Element.t
Element.scroll : ?style:Style.t -> 'msg Element.t -> 'msg Element.t

(* Identity for list reconciliation *)
Element.keyed : string -> 'msg Element.t -> 'msg Element.t

(* 2D canvas drawing *)
Element.draw : ?on_pointer_move:(pointer_event -> 'msg) -> ?on_click:(pointer_event -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_pointer_leave:'msg -> ?on_pointer_down:(pointer_event -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_pointer_up:(pointer_event -> 'msg) -> ?on_wheel:(wheel_event -> 'msg)
  -> ?cursor:Cursor.t -> ?aria_label:string
  -> width:float -> height:float -> Scene.t list -> 'msg Element.t

(* Virtualized list — renders only visible items for large datasets *)
module Virtual_list : sig
  module Positive_float : sig
    type t
    val of_float : float -> t option   (* None if <= 0 *)
    val to_float : t -> float
  end
  module Natural : sig
    type t
    val of_int : int -> t option       (* None if < 0 *)
    val to_int : t -> int
  end
  type fixed
  type 'height scroll_state
  val scroll_state : offset:float -> fixed scroll_state  (* clamps negative to 0 *)
  val offset : _ scroll_state -> float
  type range = { first : int; last : int }
  val visible_range :
    scroll_state:fixed scroll_state -> row_height:Positive_float.t ->
    container_height:Positive_float.t -> item_count:Natural.t ->
    overscan:Natural.t -> range
end

Element.virtual_list :
  ?style:Style.t -> ?on_scroll:(float -> 'msg) ->
  item_count:Virtual_list.Natural.t -> row_height:Virtual_list.Positive_float.t ->
  container_height:Virtual_list.Positive_float.t ->
  scroll_state:Virtual_list.fixed Virtual_list.scroll_state ->
  overscan:Virtual_list.Natural.t ->
  (int -> 'msg Element.t) -> 'msg Element.t

(* Message transform for component embedding *)
Element.map : ('a -> 'b) -> 'a Element.t -> 'b Element.t
```

### Style System (`nopal_style`)

All styling is typed — no CSS strings in view code.

```ocaml
(* Layout — all fields are optional; None means "no opinion" *)
type direction = Row_dir | Column_dir
type align = Start | Center | End_ | Stretch | Space_between
type size = Fill | Hug | Fixed of float | Fraction of float

type layout = {
  direction : direction option; main_align : align option;
  cross_align : align option; wrap : bool option; gap : float option;
  padding_top : float option; padding_right : float option;
  padding_bottom : float option; padding_left : float option;
  width : size option; height : size option; flex_grow : float option;
}
(* Style.default_layout has all fields None. *)

(* Colors *)
type color = Rgba of { r : int; g : int; b : int; a : float }
           | Hex of string | Named of string | Transparent

(* Borders *)
type border_style = Solid | Dashed | Dotted | No_border
type border = { width : float; style : border_style; color : color; radius : float }

(* Building styles *)
Style.default                                            (* sensible defaults *)
Style.with_layout : (layout -> layout) -> Style.t -> Style.t
Style.with_paint  : (paint -> paint)   -> Style.t -> Style.t
Style.with_text   : (Text.t -> Text.t) -> Style.t -> Style.t
Style.padding     : float -> float -> float -> float -> layout -> layout  (* T R B L, wraps in Some *)
Style.padding_all : float -> layout -> layout                             (* wraps in Some *)

Style.hex   : string -> color
Style.named : string -> color
Style.rgba  : int -> int -> int -> float -> color
```

### Interaction (`nopal_style`)

State-based style overrides for hover, press, and focus:

```ocaml
type Interaction.t = {
  hover   : Style.t option;
  pressed : Style.t option;
  focused : Style.t option;
}
```

### Typography (`nopal_style`)

```ocaml
type Font.family = System_ui | Sans_serif | Serif | Monospace | Custom of string
type Font.weight = Thin | Extra_light | Light | Normal | Medium | Semi_bold | Bold | Extra_bold | Black

type Text.t = {
  font_family    : Font.family option;
  font_size      : float option;
  font_weight    : Font.weight option;
  line_height    : line_height option;     (* Lh_normal | Lh_multiplier f | Lh_px f *)
  letter_spacing : letter_spacing option;  (* Ls_normal | Ls_em f *)
  text_align     : text_align option;      (* Align_left | Align_center | Align_right | Align_justify *)
  text_decoration: text_decoration option; (* Underline | Line_through | Overline | No_decoration *)
  text_transform : text_transform option;  (* Uppercase | Lowercase | Capitalize | No_transform *)
  text_overflow  : text_overflow option;   (* Clip | Ellipsis | Wrap | No_wrap *)
  italic         : bool option;
}
```

### UI Components (`nopal_ui`)

High-level UI components built on `nopal_element` and `nopal_style`. No platform dependencies — compiles native.

#### Toast

Dismissible toast notifications with variant-based styling and optional auto-dismiss.

```ocaml
(* Variants *)
type variant = Info | Success | Warning | Error

(* A single toast notification *)
type toast = { id : string; variant : variant; message : string }

(* Configuration for rendering a toast stack *)
type 'msg config = {
  dismiss : string -> 'msg;
  style : Style.t option;
  interaction : Interaction.t option;
  attrs : (string * string) list;
}

(* Constructor — style/interaction/attrs default to None/[] *)
val make : dismiss:(string -> 'msg) -> 'msg config

(* State helpers *)
val add : variant:variant -> message:string -> id:string -> ?duration_ms:int
  -> dismiss:(string -> 'msg) -> toast list -> toast list * 'msg Cmd.t
(* Appends a toast. When duration_ms is provided, returned Cmd schedules
   auto-dismiss via Cmd.after. Otherwise returns Cmd.none. *)

val dismiss : string -> toast list -> toast list
(* Remove toast with matching id. No-op if id not found. *)

(* View *)
val view : 'msg config -> toast list -> 'msg Element.t
(* Renders a column container. Each toast carries aria-live per variant:
   Info/Success="polite", Warning/Error="assertive". config.style controls
   container layout only — toast styling always uses default_style_for.
   Each toast is a button with on_click dispatching config.dismiss id,
   data-variant attr, and aria-label. Empty list renders empty container. *)

(* Accessibility *)
val aria_live_for : variant -> string
(* Returns "polite" for Info/Success, "assertive" for Warning/Error. *)

(* Styling *)
val default_style_for : variant -> Style.t
(* Info=blue, Success=green, Warning=amber, Error=red with padding and
   border radius. Exposed for extending rather than replacing styling. *)

val default_interaction_for : variant -> Interaction.t
(* Hover/pressed overrides per variant — each variant gets distinct hover
   and pressed background colors. Exposed for extending rather than
   replacing interaction styling. *)
```

Usage pattern — subapp integration:

```ocaml
(* Model *)
type model = { toasts : Toast.toast list; next_id : int }

(* Messages *)
type msg = ShowInfo | Dismiss of string

(* Update *)
let update model = function
  | ShowInfo ->
      let id = "toast-" ^ string_of_int model.next_id in
      let toasts, cmd =
        Toast.add ~variant:Info ~message:"Hello!" ~id ~duration_ms:3000
          ~dismiss:(fun id -> Dismiss id) model.toasts
      in
      ({ toasts; next_id = model.next_id + 1 }, cmd)
  | Dismiss id ->
      ({ model with toasts = Toast.dismiss id model.toasts }, Cmd.none)

(* View *)
let view model =
  let config = Toast.make ~dismiss:(fun id -> Dismiss id) in
  Toast.view config model.toasts
```

#### Navigation_bar

Accessible navigation bar (tablist) with active-tab tracking and optional icons.

```ocaml
(* A single navigation tab *)
type 'msg item
val item : ?icon:'msg Element.t -> id:string -> string -> 'msg item
(* [item ~id label] creates a navigation item.
   id: unique identifier used in on_select callback
   icon: optional element rendered alongside the label *)

(* Configuration for the navigation bar *)
type 'msg config
val make :
  items:'msg item list ->
  active:string ->
  on_select:(string -> 'msg) ->
  'msg config
(* [make ~items ~active ~on_select] creates a navigation bar config.
   items: non-empty list of navigation items
   active: ID of the currently active tab
   on_select: callback producing a message from the selected tab's ID *)

val with_style : Style.t -> 'msg config -> 'msg config
(* Override the container (tablist) style. *)

val with_tab_style : Style.t -> 'msg config -> 'msg config
(* Override the base style for all tab items. *)

val with_active_tab_style : Style.t -> 'msg config -> 'msg config
(* Override the style applied to the active tab (merged atop tab style). *)

val with_interaction : Interaction.t -> 'msg config -> 'msg config
(* Override hover/pressed interaction for inactive tabs. *)

val with_attrs : (string * string) list -> 'msg config -> 'msg config
(* Additional attributes on the container element. *)

val view : 'msg config -> 'msg Element.t
(* Renders the navigation bar. Container carries role="tablist".
   Each item is a Button with role="tab" and aria-selected.
   Clicking the active tab is a no-op.
   Clicking an inactive tab invokes on_select with that tab's ID. *)
```

Usage pattern — subapp integration:

```ocaml
(* Model *)
type tab = Home | Settings | About
type model = { active_tab : tab }

(* Messages *)
type msg = SelectTab of string

(* Update *)
let update _model = function
  | SelectTab id -> ({ active_tab = id_to_tab id }, Cmd.none)

(* View *)
let view model =
  let active_id = tab_to_id model.active_tab in
  let items =
    [ Navigation_bar.item ~id:"home" "Home";
      Navigation_bar.item ~id:"settings" "Settings";
      Navigation_bar.item ~id:"about" "About" ]
  in
  let config =
    Navigation_bar.make ~items ~active:active_id
      ~on_select:(fun id -> SelectTab id)
  in
  Element.column [
    Navigation_bar.view config;
    view_content model.active_tab;
  ]
```

#### Modal

Accessible modal dialog with ARIA attributes, optional backdrop, Escape-key subscription, and pure focus-cycling helper.

```ocaml
(* Configuration — opaque, constructed via make + with_* *)
type 'msg config

val make :
  open_:bool ->
  title_id:string ->
  on_close:'msg ->
  body:'msg Element.t ->
  'msg config
(* [make ~open_ ~title_id ~on_close ~body] creates a modal config.
   open_: whether the modal is currently visible
   title_id: element ID referenced by aria-labelledby
   on_close: message dispatched when Escape is pressed
   body: content rendered inside the dialog *)

val with_on_backdrop_click : 'msg -> 'msg config -> 'msg config
(* When set, renders a backdrop overlay behind the dialog. Clicking the
   backdrop dispatches the provided message. When not set, no backdrop. *)

val with_style : Style.t -> 'msg config -> 'msg config
val with_backdrop_style : Style.t -> 'msg config -> 'msg config
val with_interaction : Interaction.t -> 'msg config -> 'msg config
val with_attrs : (string * string) list -> 'msg config -> 'msg config

val view : 'msg config -> 'msg Element.t
(* When open_=false, returns Element.empty.
   When open_=true, renders:
     Box [data-testid="modal-root"]
       +- Box [data-testid="modal-backdrop"; on_pointer_down=msg]
       |     (only when on_backdrop_click is set)
       +- Column [role="dialog"; aria-modal="true";
                  aria-labelledby=title_id;
                  data-testid="modal-dialog"]
             +- body *)

val subscriptions : 'msg config -> 'msg Sub.t
(* When open_=false, returns Sub.none.
   When open_=true, returns Sub.on_keydown_prevent keyed "modal-escape"
   that intercepts Escape -> Some (on_close, true).
   All other keys are ignored (None). *)

val next_focus :
  focusable_ids:string list ->
  current:string ->
  key:string ->
  string option
(* Pure helper for Tab cycling within a modal.
   "Tab": next element after current, wrapping last->first
   "Shift+Tab": previous element before current, wrapping first->last
   Other keys: None
   Returns None if current not in list or list is empty. *)
```

Usage pattern — subapp integration:

```ocaml
(* Model *)
type model = { open_ : bool; focused : string }

(* Messages *)
type msg = OpenModal | CloseModal | FocusChanged of string

(* Update *)
let focusable_ids = [ "input-name"; "input-email"; "btn-close" ]

let update model = function
  | OpenModal -> ({ open_ = true; focused = "input-name" },
      Cmd.focus "input-name")
  | CloseModal -> ({ model with open_ = false }, Cmd.none)
  | FocusChanged id -> ({ model with focused = id }, Cmd.focus id)

(* View *)
let view model =
  let modal_config =
    Modal.make ~open_:model.open_ ~title_id:"modal-title"
      ~on_close:CloseModal
      ~body:(Element.column [
        Element.text "Dialog content";
        Element.button ~on_click:CloseModal (Element.text "Close");
      ])
    |> Modal.with_on_backdrop_click CloseModal
  in
  Element.column [
    Element.button ~on_click:OpenModal (Element.text "Open Modal");
    Modal.view modal_config;
  ]

(* Subscriptions — wire Escape + Tab trap *)
let subscriptions model =
  let modal_config =
    Modal.make ~open_:model.open_ ~title_id:"modal-title"
      ~on_close:CloseModal ~body:Element.empty
  in
  Sub.batch [
    Modal.subscriptions modal_config;
    (if model.open_ then
       Sub.on_keydown_prevent ~key:"modal-tab" (fun key ->
         match Modal.next_focus ~focusable_ids ~current:model.focused ~key with
         | Some id -> Some (FocusChanged id, true)
         | None -> None)
     else Sub.none);
  ]
```

#### Data_table

Sortable data table with ARIA table semantics and keyed rows.

```ocaml
(* Sort types *)
type sort_direction = Ascending | Descending

type sort = {
  column : string;
  direction : sort_direction;
}

(* A column definition *)
type ('row, 'msg) column = {
  header : string;
  cell : 'row -> 'msg Element.t;
  sort_key : string option;
}

(* Configuration for rendering a data table *)
type ('row, 'msg) config = {
  columns : ('row, 'msg) column list;
  rows : 'row list;
  key : 'row -> string;
  sort : sort option;
  on_sort : string -> 'msg;
  style : Style.t option;
  interaction : Interaction.t option;
  attrs : (string * string) list;
}

(* Constructor — sort_key defaults to None *)
val column : header:string -> cell:('row -> 'msg Element.t)
  -> ?sort_key:string -> unit -> ('row, 'msg) column

(* Constructor — sort/style/interaction/attrs default to None/[] *)
val make : columns:('row, 'msg) column list -> rows:'row list
  -> key:('row -> string) -> on_sort:(string -> 'msg)
  -> ?sort:sort -> ?style:Style.t -> ?interaction:Interaction.t
  -> ?attrs:(string * string) list -> unit -> ('row, 'msg) config

(* View *)
val view : ('row, 'msg) config -> 'msg Element.t
(* Renders a column container with role="table". Header row contains
   role="columnheader" cells — sortable columns wrap a button inside
   the columnheader box that dispatches on_sort with the column's
   sort_key. Active sort column carries aria-sort="ascending" or
   "descending". Data rows have role="row", cells have role="cell".
   Each data row is wrapped in Element.keyed using config.key. *)
```

Usage pattern — subapp integration:

```ocaml
(* Model *)
type model = { people : person list; sort : Data_table.sort option }

(* Messages *)
type msg = Sort of string

(* Update *)
let update model = function
  | Sort key ->
      let direction = match model.sort with
        | Some s when s.column = key && s.direction = Ascending -> Descending
        | _ -> Ascending
      in
      let sort = Some Data_table.{ column = key; direction } in
      let people = sort_by key direction model.people in
      ({ people; sort }, Cmd.none)

(* View *)
let view model =
  let config =
    Data_table.make
      ~columns:[
        Data_table.column ~header:"Name"
          ~cell:(fun p -> Element.text p.name)
          ~sort_key:"name" ();
        Data_table.column ~header:"Age"
          ~cell:(fun p -> Element.text (string_of_int p.age))
          ~sort_key:"age" ();
      ]
      ~rows:model.people
      ~key:(fun p -> p.name)
      ~on_sort:(fun key -> Sort key)
      ?sort:model.sort
      ()
  in
  Data_table.view config
```

### Routing (`nopal_router`)

Type-safe bidirectional routing:

```ocaml
Router.create : platform:(module Platform.S) -> parse:(string -> 'route option)
  -> to_path:('route -> string) -> not_found:'route -> 'route Router.t

Router.current  : 'route Router.t -> 'route                    (* read current *)
Router.push     : 'route Router.t -> 'route -> 'msg Cmd.t      (* push history *)
Router.replace  : 'route Router.t -> 'route -> 'msg Cmd.t      (* replace current *)
Router.back     : 'route Router.t -> 'msg Cmd.t                (* go back *)
Router.on_navigate : 'route Router.t -> ('route -> 'msg) -> 'msg Sub.t  (* subscribe *)
```

### Scene Types (`nopal_scene`)

Pure scene description types used by `Element.draw` and `nopal_draw`. No platform dependencies.
`nopal_element` depends on `nopal_scene` (not `nopal_draw`), keeping the element DSL free of drawing algorithms.

```ocaml
(* Re-exports: Color, Paint, Transform, Path (segment types + builders), Scene *)
(* These are also available via Nopal_draw.Color, Nopal_draw.Scene, etc. *)
```

### 2D Drawing (`nopal_draw`)

Higher-level drawing utilities built on `nopal_scene`. Re-exports all `nopal_scene` modules for convenience:

```ocaml
(* Colors — float 0.0-1.0 range *)
Color.rgba : r:float -> g:float -> b:float -> a:float -> Color.t
Color.rgb  : r:float -> g:float -> b:float -> Color.t
Color.hsla : h:float -> s:float -> l:float -> a:float -> Color.t
Color.of_hex : string -> (Color.t, string) result
Color.lerp : Color.t -> Color.t -> float -> Color.t
Color.categorical : Color.t array     (* 10 distinct colors *)

(* Paint fills *)
type Paint.t = Solid of Color.t
             | Linear_gradient of { x0; y0; x1; y1; stops }
             | Radial_gradient of { cx; cy; r; stops }
             | No_paint

(* Scene primitives *)
Scene.rect   : ?rx -> ?ry -> ?fill -> ?stroke -> x -> y -> w -> h -> unit -> Scene.t
Scene.circle : ?fill -> ?stroke -> cx -> cy -> r -> unit -> Scene.t
Scene.text   : ?font_size -> ?font_family -> ?font_weight -> ?fill -> ?anchor -> ?baseline -> x -> y -> string -> Scene.t
Scene.group  : ?opacity -> ?blend -> ?transforms -> Scene.t list -> Scene.t

(* Path building *)
Path.smooth_curve  : (float * float) list -> Path.segment list   (* Catmull-Rom spline *)
Path.straight_line : (float * float) list -> Path.segment list
Path.closed_area   : (float * float) list -> Path.segment list

(* Data-to-pixel mapping *)
Scale.create : domain:(float * float) -> range:(float * float) -> Scale.t
Scale.apply  : Scale.t -> float -> float
Scale.invert : Scale.t -> float -> float
```

### SVG Renderer (`nopal_svg`)

Pure SVG 1.1 string renderer for `nopal_scene` types. No platform deps — compiles native.

```ocaml
Nopal_svg.render : width:float -> height:float -> Scene.t list -> string
(* Renders a list of scene nodes to a complete SVG document string.
   Emits <defs> for gradients and clip paths. Output includes xmlns and viewBox. *)

(* Low-level formatting module, re-exported for unit testing *)
module Svg_fmt : sig
  type ctx
  val create_ctx          : unit -> ctx
  val color_to_css        : Color.t -> string
  val paint_to_fill_attr  : ctx -> Paint.t -> string
  val stroke_to_attrs     : ctx -> Paint.stroke -> string
  val transform_to_attr   : Transform.t -> string
  val transforms_to_attr  : Transform.t list -> string
  val path_to_d           : Path.segment list -> string
  val blend_to_css        : Scene.blend -> string
  val line_cap_to_string  : Paint.line_cap -> string
  val line_join_to_string : Paint.line_join -> string
  val render_node         : ctx -> Buffer.t -> Scene.t -> unit
  val defs_to_string      : ctx -> string
end
```

Typical usage with charts:

```ocaml
let scene = Bar.scene ~data ~label ~value ~color ~width:400.0 ~height:250.0 () in
let svg_string = Nopal_svg.render ~width:400.0 ~height:250.0 scene
```

### Charts (`nopal_charts`)

Composable interactive charts built on `nopal_draw`:

```ocaml
(* Bar chart — ?domain_window clips data when ?x is also provided *)
Bar.view : data:'a list -> label:('a -> string) -> value:('a -> float)
  -> color:('a -> Color.t) -> ?x:('a -> float) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?padding:Padding.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?format_tooltip:('a -> 'msg Element.t) -> ?on_hover:(Hover.t -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_leave:'msg -> ?hover:Hover.t -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t
  -> unit -> 'msg Element.t

(* Line chart — ?domain_window clips data with buffer=1, rescales Y *)
Line.view : series:'a Line.series list -> x:('a -> float) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?padding:Padding.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?format_tooltip:((string * float) list -> 'msg Element.t)
  -> ?on_hover:(Hover.t -> 'msg) -> ?on_leave:'msg -> ?hover:Hover.t
  -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t
  -> ?on_pointer_down:(Element.pointer_event -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_pointer_up:(Element.pointer_event -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_wheel:(Element.wheel_event -> 'msg)
  -> unit -> 'msg Element.t

(* Area chart — Stacked | Overlapping modes; ?domain_window clips with buffer=1 *)
Area.view : series:'a Area.series list -> x:('a -> float) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?mode:Area.mode -> ?padding:Padding.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?format_tooltip:((string * float) list -> 'msg Element.t)
  -> ?on_hover:(Hover.t -> 'msg) -> ?on_leave:'msg -> ?hover:Hover.t
  -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t -> unit -> 'msg Element.t

(* Pie/donut chart — inner_radius 0.0 = pie, >0.0 = donut *)
Pie.view : data:'a list -> value:('a -> float) -> label:('a -> string)
  -> color:('a -> Color.t) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?inner_radius:float -> ?label_threshold:float
  -> ?format_tooltip:('a -> 'msg Element.t) -> ?on_hover:(Hover.t -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_leave:'msg -> ?hover:Hover.t -> unit -> 'msg Element.t

(* Scatter/bubble chart — ?domain_window clips data with buffer=0 *)
Scatter.view : data:'a list -> x:('a -> float) -> y:('a -> float)
  -> ?radius:('a -> float) -> color:('a -> Color.t) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?padding:Padding.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?format_tooltip:('a -> 'msg Element.t) -> ?on_hover:(Hover.t -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_leave:'msg -> ?hover:Hover.t -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t
  -> unit -> 'msg Element.t

(* Sparkline — minimal inline chart *)
Sparkline.view : data:float list -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?color:Color.t -> ?stroke_width:float -> unit -> 'msg Element.t
```

Chart hover state: `Hover.t = { index: int; series: int; cursor_x: float; cursor_y: float }`

#### Scene Functions

Every chart module exposes a `scene` function that returns `Scene.t list` for
pure SVG export or embedding in composite scenes. Scene functions accept the
same data/layout parameters as `view` but omit interaction parameters
(`on_hover`, `on_leave`, `hover`, `format_tooltip`, event handlers).

```ocaml
Bar.scene       : data:'a list -> label:('a -> string) -> value:('a -> float)
  -> color:('a -> Color.t) -> ?x:('a -> float) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?padding:Padding.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t -> unit -> Scene.t list

Line.scene      : series:'a Line.series list -> x:('a -> float) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?padding:Padding.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t -> unit -> Scene.t list

Area.scene      : series:'a Area.series list -> x:('a -> float) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?mode:Area.mode -> ?padding:Padding.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t -> unit -> Scene.t list

Pie.scene       : data:'a list -> value:('a -> float) -> label:('a -> string)
  -> color:('a -> Color.t) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?inner_radius:float -> ?label_threshold:float -> unit -> Scene.t list

Scatter.scene   : data:'a list -> x:('a -> float) -> y:('a -> float)
  -> ?radius:('a -> float) -> color:('a -> Color.t) -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?padding:Padding.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t -> unit -> Scene.t list

Heat_map.scene  : data:'a list -> row:('a -> int) -> col:('a -> int)
  -> value:('a -> float) -> row_count:int -> col_count:int
  -> ?row_labels:string list -> ?col_labels:string list
  -> scale:Color_scale.t -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?padding:Padding.t -> unit -> Scene.t list

Sparkline.scene : data:float list -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?color:Color.t -> ?stroke_width:float -> unit -> Scene.t list

Trading.Candlestick.scene : data:'a list -> x:('a -> float)
  -> open_:('a -> float) -> high:('a -> float) -> low:('a -> float) -> close:('a -> float)
  -> width:float -> height:float -> ?padding:Padding.t
  -> ?bullish_color:Color.t -> ?bearish_color:Color.t
  -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t -> unit -> Scene.t list

Trading.Drawdown.scene : data:'a list -> x:('a -> float) -> y:('a -> float)
  -> width:float -> height:float -> ?padding:Padding.t
  -> ?fill_color:Color.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t -> unit -> Scene.t list
```

#### Heat Map

Renders a 2D grid of colored cells using `Color_scale`. Uses the accessor
pattern for API consistency with other chart types:

```ocaml
Heat_map.view : data:'a list -> row:('a -> int) -> col:('a -> int)
  -> value:('a -> float) -> row_count:int -> col_count:int
  -> ?row_labels:string list -> ?col_labels:string list
  -> scale:Color_scale.t -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?padding:Padding.t
  -> ?format_tooltip:('a -> 'msg Element.t)
  -> ?on_hover:(Hover.t -> 'msg) -> ?on_leave:'msg -> ?hover:Hover.t
  -> unit -> 'msg Element.t
```

#### Trading Namespace

Convenient grouping of trading-specific chart types:

```ocaml
module Trading : sig
  module Candlestick = Trading_candlestick
  module Drawdown = Trading_drawdown
end
```

**Candlestick chart** — OHLC candles with wicks:

```ocaml
Trading.Candlestick.view : data:'a list -> x:('a -> float)
  -> open_:('a -> float) -> high:('a -> float) -> low:('a -> float) -> close:('a -> float)
  -> width:float -> height:float -> ?padding:Padding.t
  -> ?bullish_color:Color.t -> ?bearish_color:Color.t
  -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?format_tooltip:(int -> float -> float -> float -> float -> 'msg Element.t)
  -> ?on_hover:(Hover.t -> 'msg) -> ?on_leave:'msg -> ?hover:Hover.t
  -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t -> unit -> 'msg Element.t
(* Bullish (close > open) and bearish candles use distinct colors.
   format_tooltip receives index and OHLC values. Clips via Viewport with buffer=0. *)
```

**Drawdown chart** — inverted area from 0% baseline downward:

```ocaml
Trading.Drawdown.view : data:'a list -> x:('a -> float) -> y:('a -> float)
  -> width:float -> height:float -> ?padding:Padding.t
  -> ?fill_color:Color.t -> ?x_axis:Axis.config -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> ?format_tooltip:(int -> float -> 'msg Element.t)
  -> ?on_hover:(Hover.t -> 'msg) -> ?on_leave:'msg -> ?hover:Hover.t
  -> ?domain_window:Domain_window.t -> unit -> 'msg Element.t
(* Y values should be <= 0.0 (drawdown percentages). Y domain: [min_drawdown, 0.0].
   fill_color defaults to semi-transparent red. Clips via Viewport with buffer=1. *)
```

#### Color Scale

Maps data values to colors via interpolation:

```ocaml
Color_scale.sequential : low:Color.t -> high:Color.t -> Color_scale.t
Color_scale.diverging : low:Color.t -> mid:Color.t -> high:Color.t
  -> ?midpoint:float -> unit -> Color_scale.t
Color_scale.apply : Color_scale.t -> min:float -> max:float -> float -> Color.t
(* Values outside [min, max] are clamped. Diverging midpoint defaults to 0.0. *)
```

#### Domain Window

Visible X-axis range for pan/zoom:

```ocaml
type Domain_window.t = { x_min : float; x_max : float }

Domain_window.create : x_min:float -> x_max:float -> Domain_window.t
Domain_window.equal  : Domain_window.t -> Domain_window.t -> bool
Domain_window.width  : Domain_window.t -> float
Domain_window.pan    : Domain_window.t -> delta:float -> Domain_window.t
Domain_window.zoom   : Domain_window.t -> center:float -> factor:float -> Domain_window.t
(* zoom factor < 1.0 zooms in, > 1.0 zooms out. Center maintains relative position. *)

Domain_window.clamp  : data_min:float -> data_max:float -> Domain_window.t -> Domain_window.t
(* IMPORTANT: pan/zoom do NOT clamp. Always pipe through clamp after every
   pan/zoom to prevent the window from sliding past the data range:
     Domain_window.pan dw ~delta |> Domain_window.clamp ~data_min:0.0 ~data_max:999.0
   Without clamp, the chart will go blank when the user drags past the data. *)
```

Typical pan/zoom update pattern (drag-to-pan + wheel-to-zoom + button zoom):

```ocaml
(* In your model: domain_window : Domain_window.t; drag_start_x : float option *)
let clamp_dw dw = Domain_window.clamp ~data_min:0.0 ~data_max:999.0 dw

(* Drag to pan *)
| PointerDown x -> ({ model with drag_start_x = Some x }, Cmd.none)
| PointerMove x ->
    (match model.drag_start_x with
     | None -> (model, Cmd.none)
     | Some last_x ->
         let scale = Domain_window.width model.domain_window /. chart_w in
         let delta = -.(( x -. last_x) *. scale) in
         let dw = Domain_window.pan model.domain_window ~delta |> clamp_dw in
         ({ model with domain_window = dw; drag_start_x = Some x }, Cmd.none))
| PointerUp | PointerLeave -> ({ model with drag_start_x = None }, Cmd.none)

(* Wheel to zoom — on_wheel:(fun we -> WheelZoom we) *)
| WheelZoom we ->
    let frac = we.x /. chart_w in
    let center = dw.x_min +. (frac *. Domain_window.width dw) in
    let factor = if we.delta_y > 0.0 then 1.2 else 1.0 /. 1.2 in
    let dw' = Domain_window.zoom dw ~center ~factor |> clamp_dw in
    ({ model with domain_window = dw' }, Cmd.none)

(* Button zoom — zooms around center *)
| ZoomIn ->
    let center = (dw.x_min +. dw.x_max) /. 2.0 in
    let dw' = Domain_window.zoom dw ~center ~factor:0.5 |> clamp_dw in
    ({ model with domain_window = dw' }, Cmd.none)
```

#### Chart Pane

Multi-pane synchronized layout:

```ocaml
type 'msg Chart_pane.pane = {
  height_ratio : float;
  chart : Domain_window.t -> width:float -> height:float -> 'msg Element.t;
  y_axis : Axis.config option;
}

Chart_pane.pane : height_ratio:float -> ?y_axis:Axis.config
  -> (Domain_window.t -> width:float -> height:float -> 'msg Element.t)
  -> 'msg Chart_pane.pane

Chart_pane.view : panes:'msg Chart_pane.pane list -> domain_window:Domain_window.t
  -> width:float -> height:float
  -> ?on_pointer_down:(Element.pointer_event -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_pointer_move:(Element.pointer_event -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_pointer_up:(Element.pointer_event -> 'msg)
  -> ?on_pointer_leave:'msg
  -> ?on_wheel:(Element.wheel_event -> 'msg)
  -> unit -> 'msg Element.t
(* Panes are vertically stacked; height_ratios normalized to sum to 1.0.
   Each pane's chart function receives the shared domain_window plus the
   computed pixel width and height. Raw pointer/wheel events are forwarded
   so the caller can implement pan/zoom in their update function. *)
```

#### Viewport

Clips data to visible domain window:

```ocaml
Viewport.clip : x:('a -> float) -> data:'a list -> window:Domain_window.t
  -> buffer:int -> 'a list
Viewport.clip_sorted : x:('a -> float) -> data:'a array -> window:Domain_window.t
  -> buffer:int -> 'a array
(* buffer=1 for line/area (path continuity), buffer=0 for bar/scatter.
   clip_sorted uses binary search — O(log n) vs O(n). *)
```

#### Downsample (LTTB)

Reduces large datasets while preserving visual shape:

```ocaml
Downsample.lttb : x:('a -> float) -> y:('a -> float) -> data:'a array
  -> target:int -> 'a array
Downsample.should_downsample : data_length:int -> pixel_width:float -> bool
Downsample.target_for_width  : pixel_width:float -> int
(* Threshold: 3x pixels. Target: 2x pixels. First/last points always preserved. *)
```

#### Snap

Find nearest data point for crosshair/tooltip:

```ocaml
Snap.nearest_index : x:('a -> float) -> data:'a list -> target:float -> int option
Snap.nearest_index_sorted : x:('a -> float) -> data:'a array -> target:float -> int option
(* nearest_index_sorted uses binary search — O(log n). Returns None for empty data. *)
```

#### Hit Map

Hit regions now include `Band_region` for vertical-band snapping:

```ocaml
type Hit_map.region =
  | Rect_region of { x; y; w; h; hit }
  | Circle_region of { cx; cy; r; hit }
  | Wedge_region of { cx; cy; inner_r; outer_r; start_angle; end_angle; hit }
  | Band_region of { x; w; hit }    (* vertical band, full chart height *)
```

### HTTP (`nopal_http`)

Pure HTTP types and backend abstraction. Application code uses these types in `update`; platform backends provide the actual network implementation.

```ocaml
(* Response type — includes response headers *)
type response = { status : int; body : string; headers : (string * string) list }

(* Error types *)
type error = Network_error of string | Timeout
type outcome = (response, error) result

(* Request body variants *)
type body =
  | String of { content : string; content_type : string option }
  | Json of string
  | Form_encoded of (string * string) list
  | Multipart of (string * string) list
  | Empty

(* HTTP methods and request record *)
type meth = GET | POST | PUT | DELETE | PATCH
type request = {
  meth : meth;
  url : string;
  headers : (string * string) list;
  body : body;
  timeout : float option;
}

(* Backend abstraction — set once at startup *)
type backend = { send : request -> outcome Nopal_mvu.Task.t }
type cancellable_backend = {
  send_cancellable : request -> Nopal_mvu.Task.cancellation_token * outcome Nopal_mvu.Task.t
}

val default_backend : backend                              (* always dispatches Network_error *)
val default_cancellable_backend : cancellable_backend      (* wraps default_backend with Task.cancellable *)
val register_backend : backend -> unit                     (* call at startup before mounting *)
val register_cancellable_backend : cancellable_backend -> unit

(* HTTP commands — return Cmd.t for MVU integration *)
val send : request -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> 'msg Cmd.t       (* generic, any method *)
val get : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> string -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> 'msg Cmd.t
val post : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> body:body -> string -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> 'msg Cmd.t
val put : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> body:body -> string -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> 'msg Cmd.t
val delete_ : ?body:body -> ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> string -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> 'msg Cmd.t
val patch : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> body:body -> string -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> 'msg Cmd.t

(* Cancellable variants — return token + Cmd.t *)
val send_cancellable : request -> (outcome -> 'msg)
  -> Nopal_mvu.Task.cancellation_token * 'msg Cmd.t
val get_cancellable : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> string -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> Nopal_mvu.Task.cancellation_token * 'msg Cmd.t
val post_cancellable : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> body:body -> string -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> Nopal_mvu.Task.cancellation_token * 'msg Cmd.t
val put_cancellable : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> body:body -> string -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> Nopal_mvu.Task.cancellation_token * 'msg Cmd.t
val delete_cancellable : ?body:body -> ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> string -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> Nopal_mvu.Task.cancellation_token * 'msg Cmd.t
val patch_cancellable : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> body:body -> string -> (outcome -> 'msg) -> Nopal_mvu.Task.cancellation_token * 'msg Cmd.t
```

Usage pattern in `main.ml`:

```ocaml
(* Register the web backend before mounting *)
let () =
  Nopal_http.register_backend { Nopal_http.send = Nopal_http_web.send };
  Nopal_http.register_cancellable_backend
    { Nopal_http.send_cancellable = Nopal_http_web.send_cancellable };
  Nopal_web.mount (module My_app) target
```

Usage in `update`:

```ocaml
type msg = FetchData | GotData of Nopal_http.outcome
         | Posted of Nopal_http.outcome

let update model = function
  | FetchData ->
      (model, Nopal_http.get "https://api.example.com/data" (fun r -> GotData r))
  | GotData (Ok { status = 200; body; _ }) ->
      ({ model with data = parse body }, Cmd.none)
  | GotData (Ok { status; _ }) ->
      ({ model with error = Some (Printf.sprintf "HTTP %d" status) }, Cmd.none)
  | GotData (Error (Network_error msg)) ->
      ({ model with error = Some msg }, Cmd.none)
  | GotData (Error Timeout) ->
      ({ model with error = Some "Request timed out" }, Cmd.none)
  | Posted _ -> (* handle POST response *) (model, Cmd.none)

(* POST with JSON body *)
let submit_data model =
  let body = Nopal_http.Json (encode model.form) in
  (model, Nopal_http.post ~body "https://api.example.com/data" (fun r -> Posted r))
```

### HTTP Web Backend (`nopal_http_web`)

Browser Fetch API implementation. Returns `Task.t` values — used in `main.ml` to register the backend via `{ send = Nopal_http_web.send }`. Supports all HTTP methods, headers, request bodies, and timeouts.

```ocaml
val send : Nopal_http.request -> Nopal_http.outcome Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val get : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> string -> Nopal_http.outcome Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val post : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> body:Nopal_http.body -> string -> Nopal_http.outcome Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val put : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> body:Nopal_http.body -> string -> Nopal_http.outcome Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val delete_ : ?body:Nopal_http.body -> ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> string -> Nopal_http.outcome Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val patch : ?headers:(string * string) list -> ?timeout:float
  -> body:Nopal_http.body -> string -> Nopal_http.outcome Nopal_mvu.Task.t

val send_cancellable : Nopal_http.request
  -> Nopal_mvu.Task.cancellation_token * Nopal_http.outcome Nopal_mvu.Task.t

(* All functions use the browser Fetch API. Network failures produce
   Error (Network_error msg), timeouts produce Error Timeout — never
   a raised exception. send_cancellable aborts via AbortController on cancel. *)
```

### Test Renderer (`nopal_test`)

Pure OCaml structural testing without a browser:

```ocaml
(* Render and inspect *)
Test_renderer.render   : 'msg Element.t -> 'msg rendered
Test_renderer.tree     : 'msg rendered -> node
Test_renderer.messages : 'msg rendered -> 'msg list

(* Query *)
Test_renderer.find     : selector -> node -> node option
Test_renderer.find_all : selector -> node -> node list
Test_renderer.text_content : node -> string

type selector = By_tag of string | By_text of string | By_attr of string * string
              | First_child | Nth_child of int

(* Simulate events *)
Test_renderer.click        : selector -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.input        : selector -> string -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.submit       : selector -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.dblclick     : selector -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.pointer_move : selector -> x:float -> y:float -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.pointer_leave : selector -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.pointer_down : selector -> x:float -> y:float -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.pointer_up   : selector -> x:float -> y:float -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result
Test_renderer.draw_wheel   : selector -> delta_y:float -> x:float -> y:float -> 'msg rendered -> (unit, error) result

(* Full MVU loop testing *)
Test_renderer.run_app :
  init:(unit -> 'model * 'msg Cmd.t) ->
  update:('model -> 'msg -> 'model * 'msg Cmd.t) ->
  view:('model -> 'msg Element.t) ->
  'msg list -> 'model * 'msg rendered
```

### Web Renderer (`nopal_web`)

Mount an app to the DOM (the only package with browser dependencies):

```ocaml
Nopal_web.mount :
  (module Nopal_mvu.App.S with type model = 'model and type msg = 'msg)
  -> Brr.El.t -> unit

(* Browser platform for routing *)
module Platform_web : Nopal_router.Platform.S
```

### Web Storage (`nopal_web`)

Browser localStorage access. All operations are synchronous — safe to call directly from `update`. Values persist across page reloads within the same origin.

```ocaml
Nopal_web.Storage.get    : string -> string option   (* read key from localStorage *)
Nopal_web.Storage.set    : string -> string -> unit   (* write key/value; ignores quota errors *)
Nopal_web.Storage.remove : string -> unit             (* delete key; no-op if absent *)
Nopal_web.Storage.clear  : unit -> unit               (* remove all keys *)
```

### Tauri Bindings (`nopal_tauri`)

Typed OCaml bindings to the Tauri JavaScript API for desktop/mobile targets. Depends on `nopal_mvu` for `Task.t`. All async functions return `Task.t` values — use `Cmd.task` to run them from `update`. If the Tauri runtime is not available, tasks never resolve.

```ocaml
(* App — application metadata from tauri.conf.json *)
val App.get_name          : string Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val App.get_version       : string Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val App.get_tauri_version : string Nopal_mvu.Task.t

(* Os — host platform detection via Tauri os plugin *)
type Os.platform = Windows | MacOS | Linux | IOS | Android
val Os.platform           : Os.platform Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Os.to_string          : Os.platform -> string
val Os.platform_of_string : string -> Os.platform option

(* Window — window management *)
type Window.size = { width : int; height : int }
val Window.set_title      : string -> unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.set_fullscreen : bool -> unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.is_fullscreen  : bool Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.minimize       : unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.maximize       : unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.unmaximize     : unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.is_maximized   : bool Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.close          : unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.set_size       : Window.size -> unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.inner_size     : Window.size Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.is_visible     : bool Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.show           : unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.hide           : unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.set_focus      : unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Window.center         : unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t

(* Platform — routing support for Tauri webview *)
module Platform_tauri : Nopal_router.Platform.S

(* Tray — system tray icon subscriptions + task bindings *)
(* Requires tray-icon:default capability and trayIcon in tauri.conf.json *)
val Tray.on_click       : 'msg -> 'msg Nopal_mvu.Sub.t
val Tray.on_double_click : 'msg -> 'msg Nopal_mvu.Sub.t
val Tray.set_icon       : string -> unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Tray.set_tooltip    : string -> unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Tray.set_visible    : bool -> unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t

(* Store — persistent key-value storage via tauri-plugin-store *)
(* Requires store:default capability and tauri-plugin-store registered *)
val Store.get    : string -> (string option, string) result Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Store.set    : string -> string -> (unit, string) result Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Store.delete : string -> (unit, string) result Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Store.clear  : unit -> (unit, string) result Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Store.save   : unit -> (unit, string) result Nopal_mvu.Task.t

(* Event — emit returns Task.t; listen uses callbacks (maps to Sub.custom) *)
type Event.event = { payload : string }
type Event.unlisten = unit -> unit
val Event.emit   : string -> string -> unit Nopal_mvu.Task.t
val Event.listen : string -> (Event.event -> unit) -> (Event.unlisten -> unit) -> unit
```

Usage pattern — `Cmd.task` + `Task.Syntax` composition:

```ocaml
open Nopal_mvu.Task.Syntax

(* Single task *)
| GetAppName ->
    let task = let+ name = Nopal_tauri.App.get_name in GotName name in
    (model, Cmd.task task)

(* Chained tasks *)
| ToggleFullscreen ->
    let task =
      let* fs = Nopal_tauri.Window.is_fullscreen in
      let+ () = Nopal_tauri.Window.set_fullscreen (not fs) in
      FullscreenToggled
    in
    (model, Cmd.task task)

(* Platform_tauri — routing in Tauri apps *)
let router =
  Router.create
    ~platform:(module Nopal_tauri.Platform_tauri)
    ~parse ~to_path ~not_found:Home

(* Event.listen maps to Sub.custom (not Task.t) *)
let subscriptions _model =
  Sub.custom "tauri-event" (fun dispatch ->
    Nopal_tauri.Event.listen "my-event"
      (fun ev -> dispatch (GotEvent ev.payload))
      (fun _unlisten -> ());
    fun () -> () (* cleanup *))

(* Tray — hide window to tray, restore on click *)
let subscriptions model =
  let base = My_app.subscriptions model in
  Sub.batch [ base; Nopal_tauri.Tray.on_click TrayClicked ]

| HideToTray ->
    let cmd = Cmd.task (let+ () = Nopal_tauri.Window.hide in TrayRestored) in
    (model, cmd)
| TrayClicked ->
    let cmd = Cmd.task
      (let* () = Nopal_tauri.Window.show in
       let+ () = Nopal_tauri.Window.set_focus in
       TrayRestored)
    in
    (model, cmd)

(* Store — set + save + get pattern (requires: open Nopal_mvu.Task.Syntax) *)
| SaveTheme ->
    let task =
      let* set_result = Nopal_tauri.Store.set "theme" "dark" in
      match set_result with
      | Error msg -> Task.return (SaveFailed msg)
      | Ok () ->
          let+ save_result = Nopal_tauri.Store.save () in
          match save_result with
          | Ok ()     -> ThemeSaved
          | Error msg -> SaveFailed msg
    in
    (model, Cmd.task task)

| LoadTheme ->
    let task = let+ r = Nopal_tauri.Store.get "theme" in GotTheme r in
    (model, Cmd.task task)
```

## Coding Rules

- Never `raise` for recoverable errors — return `('a, error) result`
- Never use catch-all `_` in match where the compiler can enforce exhaustiveness
- No `if/else` chains where `match` on a variant is correct
- No `mutable` fields without a justifying comment
- No inline helpers — helper functions live in dedicated modules with `.mli` files and tests
- No CSS strings in view code — all styling uses `nopal_style` types
- No business logic in `nopal_runtime` or `nopal_web` — they interpret and render only
- `App.update` never produces side effects directly — return `Cmd.t` instead
- Write tests first (TDD) — no `.ml` implementation without a failing test first
- Three test layers required: unit (Alcotest), structural (nopal_test), E2E (Playwright)
- Every PR touching runtime or web must benchmark against `bench/baseline.json` (no >20% regression)

## Project Structure

```
lib/
  nopal_mvu/         — Cmd, Sub, App — zero UI deps
  nopal_element/     — Element.t DSL + Virtual_list — zero platform deps
  nopal_style/       — Style.t, Text.t, Interaction.t, Cursor.t
  nopal_router/      — Router.t, Platform.S
  nopal_scene/       — Pure scene types: Color, Paint, Transform, Path, Scene
  nopal_draw/        — Scale + higher-level Path algorithms (re-exports nopal_scene)
  nopal_charts/      — Bar, Line, Area, Pie, Scatter, Sparkline, Heat_map,
                       Trading.Candlestick, Trading.Drawdown, Chart_pane,
                       Color_scale, Domain_window, Viewport, Downsample, Snap
  nopal_http/        — Pure HTTP types + backend abstraction
  nopal_ui/          — High-level UI components (Button, Checkbox, Data_table, Modal, Navigation_bar, Radio, Select, Toast)
  nopal_runtime/     — Lwd integration + MVU loop
  nopal_test/        — Pure OCaml test renderer
backends/
  nopal_web/         — js_of_ocaml + Brr DOM renderer
  nopal_http_web/    — Browser Fetch API backend for nopal_http
  nopal_tauri/       — Tauri desktop/mobile bindings (depends on nopal_mvu, nopal_router)
examples/
  counter/           — Hello world
  todomvc/           — Complex app with routing & localStorage
  kitchen_sink/      — Living visual reference for all features
  dashboard/         — Charts showcase
test/
  unit/              — Alcotest tests per package
  e2e/               — Playwright + TypeScript
bench/               — js-framework-benchmark + baseline.json
```

## Build Commands

```bash
just                   # build + test + fmt + lint (run before every commit)
just build             # opam exec -- dune build
just build-native      # verify DSL packages compile without js_of_ocaml
just test              # opam exec -- dune runtest
just serve-counter     # build + serve counter example
just serve-todomvc     # build + serve todomvc example
just serve-kitchen     # build + serve kitchen sink example
just serve-dashboard   # build + serve dashboard example
just bench             # run benchmark suite
just bench-compare     # compare against bench/baseline.json
```
